title: Migration v6 wrapped in single SQLite transaction is load-bearing
description: All four ALTER TABLE statements and the PRAGMA user_version = 6 run inside pool.begin() / txn.commit(). A contributor may suggest removing the transaction 'since each ALTER TABLE is auto-committed.' Without the transaction, a kill-9 between any two ALTER TABLE calls leaves orphaned columns with user_version < 6, making every subsequent open fail with 'duplicate column name.' SQLite DDL is fully transactional (unlike PostgreSQL/MySQL which have implicit DDL commits) — a rolled-back ALTER TABLE leaves no trace. The transaction is the reason the v6_migration_is_idempotent test passes.
